title: Orchestrator Verification of Stories

story.i_want_to: independently verify or reject stories that agents have reported as done
story.so_that: there is a trusted, independent assessment of whether work actually meets specifications

rationale: The verified_status track is the second half of the two-tier trust model. Only orchestrator-role keys can write to verified_status, creating a structural guarantee that agents cannot self-verify. This is the key mechanism that prevents the fabrication problem described in the PRD: agents claiming work is done when it is not. The orchestrator reads agent output, runs checks, and records its independent finding.

acceptance_criteria.description: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/verify with an orchestrator-role API key sets verified_status to 'verified', sets verified_at timestamp, and creates a verification_result record with result='pass'. Request body must include summary (text) and optionally findings (jsonb) and review_type (string).

POST /api/v1/stories/:id/reject with an orchestrator-role API key sets verified_status to 'rejected', sets rejected_at timestamp, and stores rejection_reason. Request body must include reason (text) and optionally findings (jsonb) and review_type (string). Creates a verification_result record with result='fail'.
Both verify and reject endpoints return 403 Forbidden when called with agent, user, or superadmin role API keys.
POST /api/v1/stories/:id/verify returns 409 Conflict if the story's agent_status is not 'reported_done'. Orchestrators can only verify stories that agents have actually reported as complete.
POST /api/v1/stories/:id/reject can be called on stories with agent_status 'reported_done' or verified_status 'verified' (allowing re-rejection after verification if issues are found later).

Every verify/reject action creates an audit_log entry with entity_type='story', action='verified' or 'rejected', old_state containing the previous verified_status, new_state containing the new verified_status, and the orchestrator agent identified as actor.

The verification_result record stores orchestrator_agent_id (resolved from the API key), result (pass/fail/partial), summary, findings, and review_type. It is linked to the story via story_id and scoped to tenant_id.
All endpoints enforce tenant isolation: an orchestrator in tenant A cannot verify/reject a story belonging to tenant B. Returns 404 to avoid leaking cross-tenant resource existence.
Verify and reject are atomic: the story status update, verification_result creation, and audit_log entry all succeed or fail together within a single database transaction.
POST /api/v1/stories/:id/reject requires a non-empty 'reason' field in the request body. Returns 422 Unprocessable Entity if reason is missing or blank.

test_cases.name: Happy path: verify a reported_done story
preconditions: fixture(:story, %{tenant_id: tenant.id, epic_id: epic.id, agent_status: :reported_done, verified_status: :unverified})
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/verify with body {summary: 'All artifacts present, tests pass', findings: {tests_run: 12, tests_passed: 12}, review_type: 'enhanced_review'}
Assert response 200 with verified_status=verified, verified_at != nil
expected_results: Story verified_status is 'verified' and verified_at is set
verification_result record exists with result='pass', correct summary, findings, review_type, and orchestrator_agent_id
audit_log entry exists with action='verified', old_state={verified_status: 'unverified'}, new_state={verified_status: 'verified'}

test_cases.name: Happy path: reject a reported_done story
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/reject with body {reason: 'Missing migration file', findings: {missing_artifacts: ['migration']}, review_type: 'artifact_check'}
Assert response 200 with verified_status=rejected, rejected_at != nil, rejection_reason='Missing migration file'
expected_results: Story verified_status is 'rejected', rejected_at and rejection_reason are set
verification_result record exists with result='fail' and correct summary/findings

test_cases.name: Agent-role key cannot verify or reject
expected_results: No verification_result or audit_log records created

test_cases.name: Cannot verify story that is not reported_done
expected_results: Error message indicates story must be in 'reported_done' agent_status to verify

test_cases.name: Reject without reason returns 422
expected_results: All three requests return 422 Unprocessable Entity
Error messages indicate reason is required

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: cross-tenant verification returns 404
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/:story_a_id/verify with tenant B orchestrator API key
POST /api/v1/stories/:story_a_id/reject with tenant B orchestrator API key
expected_results: Story in tenant A remains unchanged
No verification_result or audit_log records created

test_cases.name: Atomicity: failure in verification_result creation rolls back story update
preconditions: Mock verification_result insertion to raise an error
steps: Call Loopctl.Progress.verify_story/3 with valid params but mocked failure in verification_result insert

test_cases.name: Re-reject a previously verified story
steps: POST /api/v1/stories/:id/reject with body {reason: 'Found regression after initial verification', review_type: 'manual'}
expected_results: audit_log records the transition from verified to rejected

technical_notes: Implement verify_story/3 and reject_story/3 in the Loopctl.Progress context module.
Use Ecto.Multi for atomic story update + verification_result insert + audit_log insert.
Orchestrator identity is resolved from the API key's agent_id field (agent_type=orchestrator).
Controller at LoopctlWeb.StoryVerificationController with verify and reject actions.
The reject endpoint must trigger auto-reset logic (US-7.3) but that is a separate story — this story just handles the verified_status write and verification_result creation.
Verification results are immutable — each verify/reject creates a new record, building a history.
RLS policies must scope verification_results to tenant_id.
